items.title: West Ham United v Manchester United: Premier League – live
items.summary: ⚽️ Premier League updates from the 8.15pm GMT kick-off ⚽️ Live scores | Tables | Follow us on Bluesky | Email Scott That version of Bubbles with the bouncy bassline ends, the whistle goes, and Manchester United get the ball rolling. A roar from all four corners. The teams are out! West Ham United sport their famous claret and blue, while Manchester United wear their second-choice kit of “snowflake … with flashes of purple and lilac.” A rare old noise at the London Stadium ahead of a fixture that delivers, one way or another, more often than not. We’ll be off in a minute! Continue reading...

items.title: VI Live: Manchester United heeft pech, goal Casemiro afgekeurd
items.summary: Met vier wedstrijden in de Premier League en een kraker in Coppa Italia belooft het een volle voetbalavond te worden. Met VI Live blijf je op de hoogte van de laatste ontwikkelingen op de internationale velden.

items.title: Chelsea speelt Liverpool in de kaart, Spurs wint ook achtste PL-duel van 2026 niet
items.summary: Voor het eerst sinds de komst van manager Liam Rosenior heeft Chelsea punten verspeeld in de Premier League. De comeback van Leeds United op Stamford Bridge betekende goed nieuws voor Liverpool. Elders in Londen wist Tottenham Hotspur ook het achtste competitieduel van dit kalenderjaar niet om te zetten in een overwinning.

items.title: Ten-man Everton implode as Bournemouth come back to win
items.summary: Ten-man Everton imploded within the space of eight second-half minutes as they threw away a 1-0 lead at the hour mark to lose 2-1 to Bournemouth - and extend their home winless run to six games.

items.title: Report: Miami MLS' most valuable team at $1.45B
items.summary: Five clubs from MLS now have a valuation of over $1 billion, with Miami leading the way for the first time at $1.45 billion, according to a report published by Sportico on Tuesday.

items.title: Slot: 'Liverpool unlucky?' after Jacquet injury
items.summary: Arne Slot has said Liverpool's luck this season has been so bad it has even extended to new signing Jérémy Jacquet, who has suffered a shoulder injury.

items.title: Bayern: Kane in 'intensive talks' over future
items.summary: Bayern Munich remain in "intensive talks" with Harry Kane over his future, according to the club's sporting director Max Eberl.

items.title: Sources: Cristiano Ronaldo ends Al Nassr dispute
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo has decided to end his boycott of Al Nassr games, sources have told ESPN Brasil.

items.title: Positie Jans geen thema voor El Karouani: 'Er zit een stijgende lijn in'
items.summary: De druk op trainer Ron Jans neemt toe nu FC Utrecht al negen Eredivisie-duels op rij niet heeft gewonnen, maar volgens Souffian El Karouani bestaat er in de spelersgroep geen enkele twijfel over de positie van de 67-jarige trainer.

items.title: Sporting spreekt schande van sabotage Porto in topper en stapt naar bond
items.summary: Dat er veel op het spel stond bij de topper tussen FC Porto en Sporting Portugal was niet alleen te zien op het veld. Ook daaromheen werd er aan allerlei knoppen gedraaid om elkaar het leven zuur te maken. Het krijgt mogelijk nog een staartje, want Sporting heeft aangekondigd naar de Portugese voetbalbond (FPF) te stoppen.

items.title: Women’s Champions League playoffs to test WSL teams’ depth and new format’s value
items.summary: Arsenal and Manchester United face familiar opponents in a revamped competition that has so far proven more competitive The next phase of the new Women’s Champions League format gets under way on Wednesday and Thursday, when the four playoff first legs take place. Arsenal and Manchester United are among eight teams vying to join Barcelona, Lyon, Chelsea and Bayern Munich, who automatically qualified for the quarter-finals as the top four in the league phase. As in the men’s competition, a switch from a group stage to the league format has been made. However, with the UWCL group stage having been introduced only for the 2021-22 season, there was scepticism about a change coming so soon, with the group stage viewed as a big step forward and in its relative infancy. Continue reading...

items.title: Van Barneveld records his lowest-ever average on PDC ProTour as Nijman wins epic
items.summary: Raymond van Barneveld recorded his lowest-ever average on the PDC ProTour in a 6-1 defeat to Owen Bates in the second round of Players Championship Two on Tuesday.

Stokes undergoes successful surgery on eye injury
items.summary: England Test captain Ben Stokes has undergone successful surgery on an eye injury he suffered after being struck by a cricket ball earlier this month.

items.summary: Fabio Wardley is finalising terms for a WBO heavyweight title defence against Daniel Dubois on May 9.

items.title: Football Daily | Drama, twists and unbearable tension: the Championship is in a league of its own
items.summary: Sign up now! Sign up now! Sign up now? Sign up now! Very few football fanbases have experienced a more dramatic decade than supporters of Coventry City. Nine years ago, the club slipped into League Two in front of four-digit crowds at the cavernous Ricoh Arena. They managed to rebound under Mark Robins, despite constant uncertainty under owners Sisu, and a spell in exile at Birmingham City’s ground for their Championship return in 2020. Three years later, a playoff final between two comeback clubs went the way of Luton, on penalties. And last season, the chief suit, Doug King, decided to replace Robins with Frank Lampard, an unpopular decision justified by a push for promotion that ended in an unlucky playoff semi-final defeat to Sunderland. Re: ‘Late but well worth it’ (yesterday’s last line, full email edition). Hold-ups on the line. An agonisingly slow tube journey. Dad and I arriving at Brisbane Road well after kick-off on 8 September 1962. Proudly kitted out in my blue scarf knitted by mum, with my blue rattle carpentered by dad (yes, we played in blue then). Barely time to grab a rosette, a hot dog and a bag of roast chestnuts. ‘Hurry up son,’ says the friendly turnstile man. ‘You don’t want to miss this one.’ Nearly 25,000 standing fans crammed into the tiny ground, but generously parting to allow us to move nearer the pitch. Still 0-0 at half-time, Denis Law kept at bay. Floodlights now casting lengthening shadows in the smoky, onion-scented air, cigarettes twinkling across the pitch. Eighty-nine minutes, still no score, then Orient winger Terry McDonald grabs the goal that consigns him to the local pantheon and Matt Busby’s boys to a 1-0 defeat. We may have ended up one-season wonders, but what a day that was” – Max Maxwell. Erling Haaland, Patron Saint of First World Problems, was right to dedicate the win at Anfield to long-suffering City fans cheering ‘week in, week out’ (yesterday’s News, Bits and Bobs, full email edition). To put their almost-18-month trophy drought into context, a child born the day they won the 2024 Community Shield would today have only marginally more hair and decorum than Ian Holloway” – Rowan Sweeney. Mike Kilner was the only person who bothered to write a letter (yesterday’s Football Daily letter) and still didn’t win letter o’ the day. Maybe if this letter is published and wins the letter of the day, he can live vicariously through me” – Nigel Sanders. Continue reading...
